channel.title: Foundation Universe (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Foundation Universe" - Discussion of things relating to both the SCP objects and the "universe" of the Foundation as a whole.

item.title: Does Spicy Crust Pizza have an email address?
item.content:encoded: I'm making a skip and it involves the Foundation Front company, Spicy Crust Pizza. I'm wondering if that company has an official email in-universe. If there is one, do you know what the name of it is?

item.title: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9
item.description: I'm a little confused, and a little lazy. Can someone explain these two to me?
item.content:encoded: I've been working on an idea for an SCP article and while trying to brainstorm ideas for it I looked at the MTF squads and noticed Omega-7 and Alpha-9. I don't really have the time or the will to read through both of these and everything about them, so could someone give me a rundown on the two? Like what's the differences between the two, how they came about, who's invovled, and most importantly would Last Hope be where ALL humaniod SCP's the Foundation is trying to use in the field or just some?

item.title: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.
item.content:encoded:
We did believe reality benders can warp anything they live respectively of the universe/dimension they reside in, but i never think they could achieve crossing dimensions from the thought of anomalous limits. 507 is not a reality bender since he has just a displacement effect yet to my question:

is it possible you can use anomaly people like him along with reality benders, stick em in dimensional experiment machines and use them as a form of a dimensional energy to power a vehicle that can cross through the other ever expanding dimensional worlds?

item.title: Hearts and minds?
item.description: To what extent does the Foundation care about hearts and minds, or morality in general?
item.content:encoded:
Now, I know the obvious answer is no, they don't care about hearts and minds. The Foundation is an organization based on the utmost secrecy and regular commits atrocious acts against relatively innocent civilians, so outside of the Broken Masquerade canon they don't concern themselves with a public image.

But what I'm asking about is do they care about the hearts and minds of their own people? Many articles suggest they do to some extent. The entire point of SCP-231 is how far the Foundation is willing to go and the psychological toll it takes on their personnel. So to what extent does morality dictate their actions?

Suppose an MTF is on a mission and one of their guys gets grabbed by something. Mission Control can tell from his vitals that he's injured but still alive, maybe he's even calling for help over the radio. I feel like on any ordinary military mission, rescuing an injured teammate would take priority, but if the Foundation sent in a team then chances are they're already prepared to lose them all. So what would they do? Would they shut off the downed agent's radio and instruct the team to continue the mission? Can they expect their agents to be loyal if they know the Foundation won't bother to save them if they get in trouble? If there are any articles that feature a situation like this, please feel free to tell me, I'd love to read them.

And another thing, do SCP objects that are more subjectively "evil" get treated as higher risks? For example, would an entity like SCP-4666 that targets children be a higher priority than other uncontained SCPs simply because humans are more protective of their young?

I apologize if this is a conversation that's been had before, but the internal morality of the Foundation is a subject that fascinates me, and I'd love to hear people's thoughts on it. Thanks!

item.title: Trying to find a skip I read a while ago
item.description: I can only remember a few details so I was hoping someone might know what I'm looking for!
item.content:encoded: The skip in question involved a closed off road to some woods, and once you went in the road and terrain would get more and more alien/jagged/whatever, making it impossible to get that far. Then a truck with some figure inside would start chasing you. I think it may have mentioned the figure looking like a middle-aged man. If you tried to run it wouldn't work, and instead of just killing I remember it wrapped the MTF team in something that made them fall up and be caught on a telephone wire (or something) kind of like a spider, and then the log ends. That's all I really remember, so if anyone has any idea I'd love to know its kind of bugging me.

item.title: Is there a Seth
item.description: Got to love that Original Sin stuff.
item.content:encoded: In the Foundation Universe, there are two SCP's that have always drawn my attention. SCP-073 and SCP-076-2. Both have strong implications that they are Cain and Abel from the Book Of Genesis. Now, in the Foundation Universe, I've seen some evidence of other Genesis characters, such as Adam and Eve (O5-1) and Lilith. However, I can't find any evidence of Seth, the third son of A&E. Is there an SCP or Tale involving him or has he yet to enter/be discovered in the Foundation World?

item.title: SCP Number Assignment
item.description: What is the canon behind the assignment of numbers to SCP?
item.content:encoded:
What is the canon behind the assignment of numbers to objects?
The foundation has a large number of objects that they contain/monitor with some files being as recent as a year while others dating back decades. There seems to be no particular chronological order to the numbers assigned to a given SCP (ie a 5th series scp could be in the foundations possession since the 70's while one of the original 1st series could only date back to the early 2000's . Now obviously this just a by product of how the out-of-universe site operates, but I was wondering if there was an in-universe explanation for this. Any additional info on the subject is welcome as well :)

item.title: What makes the Foundation the Foundation?
item.description: A very dumb question I know.
item.content:encoded: What is the main core of the Foundation? sure we have the O5 council but they can't run the entire foundation with all these new anomalies all around the world by themselves(probably). The researchers don't run the foundation since they only know what's in their current clearance level. So what makes the foundation once you strip away the O5 council and the researchers? Chaos all around the world? or would the essence of the foundation keep the anomalies at bay?

item.title: Some famous PoIs?
item.content:encoded:
Can you guys list some famous PoIs and short description about them?
Btw, when I search character tags. I found a tag called kindness. So what is that?

item.title: Different Sectors and site specialty's
item.content:encoded: I know there’s a list on the scp wiki but to be brutally honest it seems pretty vague to me for instance a bio site could mean a number of things so i was wondering is there a known list of all the different sectors or sites that contain certain types of scp’s for instance zoological sites for megafauna or Humanoid sites for more younger Entity's

item.title: Canada based SCP sites?
item.content:encoded:
Are there any SCP Sites in Canada? if so where are they located?
Also, if Timmins Ontario is not currently a location yet, given the mass amounts of closed mines in the area it would be a good location for an underground site or ever as few…

item.title: Idea to prevent covid (In the SCP universe that is)
item.content:encoded: If it cures all diseases. Just give it to everyone, the foundation can set up stations where every person that is infected gets the pill and his problems are over. No need for selling it just giving it to the infected. Problem solved but that is just my idea. I know this is a huge time waster but if you have any opinions I would like to read them. Always interesting when other people comment on crazy and or stupid ideas.

item.title: Information on the IS and IS NOT entity from SCP-4840?
item.content:encoded: I'm interested on the IS and IS NOT entities from the old man's story in 4840. Is there any other tales or scp about theme. Is there a lore?

item.title: Who are the four Archangels in Scp Foundation?
item.description: I am theorizing about Scp-001 the gate guardian, Scp-343, 990 and maybe 738 & 469. Also a project of an angel Scp I am planning to write about.
item.content:encoded:
I think 001 the Gate Guardian maybe Uriel, 990 maybe Gabriel, since he helps foundation through dreams. 343 too, is an angel from actual God in Scp universe, to test humanity's faith if they'll think of him as their actual creator.

They are all selfless, pure beings without flaws or their own decisions, so 001 doesn't move out of the door of Eden, 990 doesn't seem to be anywhere except for dreams who inform foundation personel, 343 plays his role as a fake God identity.

(Maybe 738 is actually Satan? Which makes him an ex-angel? Or I guess there are multiple Satans in Scp Universe, this can be explained through his paranormal/demonic powers of being in multiple places at once. -but that's my guess)

I am not sure about 469 tho, maybe it is just a low ranked angel.

There is a Scp I am planning to write about tho, that'll be the final archangel, other than that, do you guys know if there are other angel Scps?

item.title: What is the black moon?
item.description: I keep hearing it everywhere but I dont know what it means
item.content:encoded: What is it, and why is it referenced to howling i.e. ¨Ẅhy does the black moon howl¨Any theories or ideas on what this means?

item.title: Can someone explain the entirety of the Scarlet King lore?
item.description: I've read a few main pieces of the series such as Tufto's Proposal, 231/7, and KTE-2013-Kappala-Mendes but I want to build up on the lore
item.content:encoded: I find the Scarlet King lore really interesting and per summery I have read the main few pieces of it including SCP-2317, Tufto's Proposal, SCP-231-7, and KTE-2013-Kappala Mendes. However, how could one theoretically build upon this lore or learn more about it?

item.title: apollyon class scps
item.description: Do you think the object class Apollyon was a good idea, why or why not?
item.content:encoded: I've read every single apollyon class because personally I find them interesting HOWEVER it is a huge double-edged sword as it's so powerful it's cliche. For example, a few of them I found a personal favorite such as S. D. Locke's proposal and SCP-5055. Do you find them interesting? Why or why not? If so, what are your favorites?

item.title: Multiversal Collapse
item.content:encoded: How many times has the god damn Foundation dealt with a Secret wars 2015 level kind of shit with the multiverse?

item.title: How do Type Greens come to be?
item.description: I'm writing a story about a reality bender, but what I'm wondering is how they develop their skills. How do they get them, and if so, why?
item.content:encoded: I'm thinking it's inherited somehow, but I'm still not sure. Is it a contagion? I know it depends on our headcanons, but I'm just looking for a more definite answer or any tales or skips with more information about it.

item.title: A problem with alot of scp articles.
item.content:encoded:
Input: 1 chimp (setting: Fine)

Output: [DATA EXPUNGED]

Input: 1 chimp (setting: Rough)

Output: Badly mutilated corpse, showing signs of crushing and cutting with high heat

Those are lines from SCP-914. Why would the SCP use 'chimp' instead of chimpanzee?
A lot of SCPs are structured like poorly-written wikipedia articles, instead of actual scientific reports.
Censored information makes no sense either, why would it be censored in the first place?
Honestly, draft-reviewers should be more informed of things like this.
